prompt: The method should compute n choose k but gives wrong results for large inputs. What is the bug?
choices: The base case k == n should be k >= n
The plain recursive approach has exponential time complexity due to overlapping subproblems; for large n it is extremely slow or overflows the call stack
nCk(n-1,k-1) should be nCk(n,k-1) per Pascal's triangle
The return type long cannot store combinatorial results
explanation: The naive recursive implementation recomputes the same nCk(i,j) values exponentially many times. For n=40, k=20 this is billions of calls. Fix: use dynamic programming (bottom-up table) or add memoization with a HashMap to cache previously computed values.
